review_notes: The functions generally map well to the FedRAMP SC-7 (3) control, focusing on boundary protection and access point security. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but there are minor improvements that could enhance clarity and consistency.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' to 'k8s_service_nodeport_avoidance' for better clarity and alignment with the action being checked.
title: Boundary Protection | Access Points
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP SC-7 (3) control focuses on boundary protection and ensuring that access points are secure. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network security controls such as NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that only authorized traffic is allowed. Additionally, securing ingress points with TLS and avoiding the use of NodePort services, which expose services directly to the internet, align with the control's intent to protect access points.
